## Run-Mode Snapshot

| field | value |
|---|---|
| active mode | standard integration |
| rollout type | worktree-to-main integration and reconciliation |
| gap-closure mode | no; this is not gap-closure mode and must not reopen already-closed product scope |
| implementation posture | import, reconcile, or skip already-present worktree row deltas; do not reimplement rows from scratch |
| original plan posture | reuse existing worktree row plans and closure evidence as historical source-of-truth; do not recreate, rewrite, or rerun original implementation plans |
| allowed future plan type | only a minimal integration contract for importing or verifying a specific row in main |
| target checkout | `/Users/I560101/Project-Sat/mknoon-2/flutter_app` |

## Recommended Plan Count

Recommended plan count: 202 minimal integration contracts.

This count includes 202 covered/importable worktree rows after the updated source worktree closure of `PL-002`, `ML-012`, and `ST-014`. It excludes `PREREQ-MULTI-PARTY-DEVICE-HARNESS` because that is a shared prerequisite, not a source matrix row.

## Decomposition Artifact

- integration breakdown: `Test-Flight-Improv/Group-Chat-Feature/Private_group_chat_reliability_test_matrix_full_with_rules-worktree-to-main-integration-session-breakdown.md`
- source worktree breakdown: `/Users/I560101/Project-Sat/mknoon-2/worktrees/full-rules-pipeline/Test-Flight-Improv/Group-Chat-Feature/Private_group_chat_reliability_test_matrix_full_with_rules-session-breakdown.md`
- source worktree matrix: `/Users/I560101/Project-Sat/mknoon-2/worktrees/full-rules-pipeline/Test-Flight-Improv/Group-Chat-Feature/Private_group_chat_reliability_test_matrix_full_with_rules.md`
- target main checkout: `/Users/I560101/Project-Sat/mknoon-2/flutter_app`
- main compatibility artifact: `/Users/I560101/Project-Sat/mknoon-2/flutter_app/Test-Flight-Improv/Group-Chat-Feature/Private_group_chat_reliability_test_matrix_COMPLETE_1-session-breakdown.md`

## Source Of Truth

The worktree row entry, source matrix row, worktree row plan file, and worktree closure/evidence notes are historical evidence for work that already happened. They must be inspected and reused as input. They must not be recreated, rewritten, or treated as prompts for a new implementation rollout.

For row `R`, the historical worktree plan file is:

`/Users/I560101/Project-Sat/mknoon-2/worktrees/full-rules-pipeline/Test-Flight-Improv/Group-Chat-Feature/Private_group_chat_reliability_test_matrix_full_with_rules-session-R-plan.md`

If a future minimal integration contract is useful, it must be doc-scoped and named:

`Test-Flight-Improv/Group-Chat-Feature/Private_group_chat_reliability_test_matrix_full_with_rules-worktree-to-main-integration-session-INTEGRATE-R-plan.md`

## Integration Session Contract

Every `INTEGRATE-*` session must:

1. Inspect the worktree row entry in the source breakdown and source matrix.
2. Inspect the existing worktree row plan file plus closure/evidence notes.
3. Inspect overlapping or conflicting rows in the main COMPLETE_1 breakdown and their plan files.
4. Identify exact code, test, and doc files changed by that worktree row before editing main.
5. Compare whether those changes are already present in main.
6. Integrate only the missing meaningful row-owned delta into main.
7. Avoid duplicate tests, code, helpers, docs, harness changes, and broad formatting already present in main.
8. Never reimplement a row from scratch.
9. If the row is already present in main, mark the integration session `skipped_already_present` with concrete evidence.
10. If only part is present, integrate only the missing meaningful delta and name what was skipped.
11. If conflicts exist, stop that row and map affected rows from both breakdowns before resolving.
12. Run the worktree row's focused tests plus affected COMPLETE_1/main tests before accepting.
13. Update this integration ledger with exactly one final status: `accepted`, `skipped_already_present`, `blocked_conflict`, or `blocked_external_fixture`.

## Known COMPLETE_1 Overlap Guard

These overlap mappings are mandatory starting points, not the full overlap set. Each session must also discover overlaps from exact changed files, scenario language, test names, helper names, and harness surfaces.

| worktree row | COMPLETE_1 rows to inspect first | risk |
|---|---|---|
| `ML-006` | `GM-005`, `GM-018`, `GM-020` | High |
| `ML-007` | `GM-006`, `GM-007`, `GM-008`, `GM-019`, `GM-021`, `GM-024` | High |
| `ML-008` | `GM-022`, `GM-009`, `GM-010`, `GM-024` | High |
| `ML-009` | `GM-011`, `GM-012`, `GM-013`, `GM-014` | High |
| `ML-011` | `GM-009` and related stale-ordering rows | High |
| `KE-005` | `GL-015` | High |

Shared proof surfaces that must trigger duplicate checks include `./scripts/run_test_gates.sh groups`, `./scripts/run_test_gates.sh completeness-check`, `integration_test/group_multi_party_device_real_harness.dart`, `integration_test/scripts/run_group_multi_party_device_real.dart`, `integration_test/scripts/group_multi_party_device_criteria.dart`, `test/integration/group_multi_party_device_criteria_test.dart`, group membership/messaging/recovery Flutter tests, and Go group inbox/pubsub/envelope tests.

## Row Traceability Rule

Every represented source row maps to exactly one integration session id, `INTEGRATE-<source-row-id>`. No completed/importable worktree row may be merged into a broad subsystem session. Later reconciliation must report final truth per source row, not only per shared harness, file, or COMPLETE_1 row family.

## Ordered Session Breakdown

Run sessions in the exact order of the Session Ledger above. That ledger order is the ordered session breakdown for this integration artifact.

Each `INTEGRATE-*` session has the same execution shape:

- scope: import or reconcile exactly one worktree row into main.
- input evidence: worktree source matrix row, worktree source breakdown row/session entry, historical worktree row plan, and closure/evidence notes.
- main compatibility check: COMPLETE_1 breakdown row search by scenario, files, test names, helper names, and known overlap guard.
- changed-file inventory: exact code/test/doc file list reconstructed from worktree evidence before any edit.
- integration action: accept missing meaningful row-owned delta, skip already-present work, or block with conflict/external-fixture evidence.
- verification: worktree row focused tests plus affected COMPLETE_1/main tests.
- ledger update: one of `accepted`, `skipped_already_present`, `blocked_conflict`, or `blocked_external_fixture`.

`PL-002`, `ML-012`, and `ST-014` are ordered importable sessions with covered source-worktree evidence. Future integration passes must still inspect their worktree row entries, historical plans, closure evidence, changed-file inventories, and COMPLETE_1 overlaps before importing or skipping them; they must not recreate the original implementation plans or invent local implementation work.

## Downstream Execution Path

1. Pick the first ledger row whose status is `pending_integration`.
2. Create only a minimal integration contract if needed; do not regenerate the original implementation plan.
3. Inspect the worktree row, historical plan, closure notes, source matrix row, and COMPLETE_1 overlaps.
4. Build the exact worktree changed-file inventory for that row.
5. Compare each file and behavior against main.
6. If already present, mark `skipped_already_present` with evidence.
7. If partially present, integrate only the missing meaningful delta.
8. If conflicting, stop row work and map affected rows from both breakdowns before resolving.
9. Run focused row tests plus affected COMPLETE_1/main tests.
10. Update the ledger and move to the next row.

## Final Verdict Policy

Final verdict is `closed` only when every integration session has one final status: `accepted`, `skipped_already_present`, `blocked_conflict`, or `blocked_external_fixture`, and every non-blocked accepted row records exact files and tests. The verdict is `still_open` while any row remains `pending_integration`. The verdict is `blocked` if any row is `blocked_conflict` without a mapped resolution path or if a newly discovered external fixture blocker prevents import verification.

This artifact remains the controlling integration breakdown and is updated as rows are imported, skipped, or blocked one session at a time.
